source_mentions.role_label: chief executive officer of the Ritter Center

cross_domain_targets.summary: The official December 14, 2023 implementation plan names Ritter Center as one of the City's nonprofit partners administering the needs-assessment and outreach event.

not_promoted: No direct contract, grant, or campaign/disclosure join for Ritter Center is promoted from this example.

cross_domain_join_examples.notes: This is a valid cross-domain join even without a contract object because it links a quoted recurring actor to an organization and then to an official program-partner record.

cross_domain_targets.summary: The official implementation plan names Downtown Streets Team as one of the City's nonprofit partners for the December 2023 needs assessment and outreach event.
Derived contract record for the FY 2024-25 Downtown Streets Team agreement.
$200,000 contract authorization, with $40,000 ERF3 grant portion and $160,000 FY 2024-25 general-fund portion.
City Council resolution authorizing the first phase of the sanctioned-camping program and related agreements.

accepted_links: media label Downtown Streets Team -> actor-downtown-streets-team

cross_domain_join_examples.notes: This is the cleaner contract-side pattern: the same organization label is stable across media, official program context, the derived contract record, the money flow, and the authorizing decision.

methodology_findings.summary: Cross-domain joins do not all terminate in the same object class. Some should stop at organization-plus-program-context, while others can continue through contract, money, and decision objects.
Media recurrence is most useful when it points into already normalized official records. The media layer should not be forced to invent the downstream civic objects itself.
A quoted person can anchor a cross-domain join without implying that the person controls every downstream program or contract linked to the affiliated organization.

notes: This file extends the media layer into organization, program, contract, moneyflow, and decision joins.
It intentionally keeps the Shotwell/Ritter example narrower than the Downtown Streets Team example because the underlying official record shape is narrower.
